whatItMeasures: Folate (vitamin B9) is essential for DNA synthesis, cell division, and the formation of red blood cells; this test measures folate in the blood (serum or plasma) and is used to diagnose folate deficiency anaemia and monitor folate status.
causesOfHigh: Diet very high in folate-rich foods (leafy greens, legumes)
Vitamin B12 deficiency can cause serum folate to appear elevated (folate cannot enter cells without B12)
causesOfLow: Poor dietary intake (low consumption of leafy greens, legumes, fortified foods)
Alcohol use disorder (alcohol impairs folate absorption and increases excretion)
Pregnancy (increased folate demand — low folate is associated with neural tube defects in the foetus)
causesOfLowSource: NHS. Vitamin B12 or folate deficiency anaemia. https://www.nhs.uk/conditions/vitamin-b12-or-folate-deficiency-anaemia/

high: High folate from supplementation causes no symptoms
Very high supplemental folate can mask vitamin B12 deficiency neurological symptoms
low: Shortness of breath and rapid heartbeat (megaloblastic anaemia)
Neural tube defects in the newborn if deficient in early pregnancy

symptomsSource: NHS. Vitamin B12 or folate deficiency anaemia — symptoms. https://www.nhs.uk/conditions/vitamin-b12-or-folate-deficiency-anaemia/symptoms/

citations.title: Vitamin B12 or folate deficiency anaemia
